summary: Implemented the minimal M5 playable client flow as a protocol/read-model driven UI slice. The player can start the accepted M5 slice, inspect goals and success/failure conditions, preview and confirm protocol commands through the accepted M5 command script, see command results, cancel/failure preview, save/load a client replay checkpoint, and inspect AI reasons, campaign risk, supply, season, automatic battle/siege/withdrawal evidence, and postwar consequences. React stores only UI session progress and read-model slices; Pixi remains a map render cache; no sim-core, sim-ai, save-format, Electron, telemetry, server, multiplayer, account, branding, release, or manual battle scope was added.

tests_run.result: On feat/m5-client-playable-ui; modified allowed implementation/test files plus this task/handoff after final handoff creation.
1 test file passed; 14 tests passed, including M5 playable read-model, command preview, save/load parser, malformed save rejection, and no WorldState leak checks.
1 test file passed; 6 tests passed, including M5 playable workspace SSR coverage.
1 test file passed; 2 tests passed; existing map read-model delta/cache rebuild coverage remains green.
1 test file passed; 4 tests passed; web shell snapshot now includes M5 playable read model and no WorldState leak.
1 test file passed; 9 tests passed; protocol schema regression remains green.

tests_run.command: pnpm --filter @monsoon/client-core typecheck && pnpm --filter @monsoon/ui typecheck && pnpm --filter @monsoon/map-renderer typecheck && pnpm --filter web typecheck

tests_run.result: All matched files use Prettier code style after formatting the changed files.
Web production build passed; Vite reported the existing large chunk warning.
Storybook build passed using the repository's actual script name; the existing WebClientShell story now includes the M5 playable workspace. Vite reported the existing large chunk warning.
Repository has no build:storybook script; pnpm suggested build:web. Actual Storybook build command is pnpm storybook:build and it passed.

8 Playwright tests passed, including M5 start-to-finish, save/load, preview/confirm/result, AI reason display, postwar display, failure/cancel flow, 4000-row stress continuity, and no horizontal overflow at 1280x720, 1280x800, 1920x1080, and 2560x1080.
Repository has no e2e script. Actual e2e command is pnpm test:e2e and it passed.

decisions: Implemented M5 client progression as UI session state plus read-model slices over createM5PlayableLoopScriptV1, not as client-owned WorldState or direct sim mutation.

Client save/load is a replay checkpoint DTO containing scenario id, phase, current command index, confirmed command ids, checkpoint label, and state hash; it is intentionally not an authoritative save-format envelope.
Raised the single-column responsive breakpoint to 1320px so 1280x720 and 1280x800 acceptance viewports avoid horizontal overflow with dense M3/M4/M5 panels.
Kept manual node battle visible only as out-of-scope text/reason evidence; no manual battle UI or tactical controls were added.

risks.risk: Storybook normal-state coverage is provided through the existing root WebClientShell story. The task allowed apps/storybook/**, but this repository's active Storybook config lives under .storybook, which was outside the allowed_paths for this task, so no new dedicated normal/empty/error/extreme/narrow story file was added.

M5 client flow submits and displays protocol command DTOs and checkpoint evidence, but authoritative browser worker execution of the full M5 loop remains outside this client-only implementation unless a later task exposes an accepted worker adapter to apps/web.
Web and Storybook builds pass with the pre-existing large chunk warning.

requested_action: Review M5-CLIENT-PLAYABLE-UI-001 for acceptance criteria, especially client/read-model authority boundaries, M5 command preview/confirm/result behavior, save/load checkpoint semantics, Playwright viewport/no-overlap evidence, Storybook coverage limitation, and absence of manual battle/Electron/telemetry/server scope.
